constraints.layout_style: nested sections with h3 headings instead of labels

required_complexity_patterns.notes: Inputs laid out in table cells, not flex card stacks only
Newsletter or login decoy form separate from job application form
Label wraps input through nested divs without for attribute
Multiple controls sharing name attribute groups (checkbox/radio clusters)
Custom text field: div with role=textbox and contenteditable, no native input

prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real ashby-style job application (single-page, small, tier=high). Widgets: combobox. Required: Inputs laid out in table cells, not flex card stacks only Required: Newsletter or login decoy form separate from job application form Required: Label wraps input through nested divs without for attribute Required: Multiple controls sharing name attribute groups (checkbox/radio clusters) Required: Custom text field: div with role=textbox and contenteditable, no native input Reference DOM (Fake role textbox div): Custom component: div with role=textbox, no native input Reference DOM (Contenteditable name field): contenteditable div poses as a text input for full name Reference DOM (Ashby yes no buttons): Ashby-inspired yes/no as two styled buttons instead of native radio Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: table-layout, decoy-form, label-wraps-deep, duplicate-name-attrs, div-role-textbox. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: nested sections with h3 headings instead of labels. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Fake role textbox div; Contenteditable name field; Ashby yes no buttons.
